title: task
description:
edhoc.py: Multiple locations access CBOR sequence items without validating minimum length, causing IndexError on malformed inputs instead of clear protocol errors.

Locations:
- Line 297-299: process_message_2() accesses items[0], items[1] without checking len(items) >= 2
- Line 326-327: Falls back to empty bytes for missing PLAINTEXT_2 fields, leading to confusing signature verification errors downstream
- Line 507-511: process_message_1() accesses items[1], items[2], items[3] without checking len(items) >= 4  
- Line 593-595: process_message_3() accesses pt3_items[0], pt3_items[1] without checking len(pt3_items) >= 2

Failure scenario: A malformed or truncated EDHOC message causes IndexError ('list index out of range') instead of a clear ValueError('Malformed EDHOC message - missing required fields'). This obscures the real problem during debugging and could affect error handling in callers.

Fix: Add explicit length checks before accessing sequence items, raise ValueError with descriptive messages.
close_reason: Duplicate: Already fixed in bead zkbg - all CBOR sequence length checks are in place
